parameters.name: subdivide
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: Subset of input to use as a polygonal mesh to subdivide. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: creases
parameters.label: Creases
parameters.description: This field allows you to specify a subset of the second input to use as creases. The elements of the second input specified by the field are used as creases. Each edge in a crease polygon corresponds

parameters.name: iterations
parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description: How many iterations to subdivide. Higher numbers give a smoother surface.

parameters.name: overridecrease
parameters.label: Override Crease Weight Attribute
parameters.description: Determine if the crease sharpness should be determined by the primitive or vertex attribute or by overridden by this .

parameters.name: creaseweight
parameters.label: Crease Weight
parameters.description: If the crease weight is overriden, this is the weight used.

parameters.name: outputcrease
parameters.label: Generate Resulting Creases
parameters.description: If any creases are sharper than the , they will be left over in the resulting geometry. With this parameter enabled, these left over creases are created with the appropriate primitive attribute values

parameters.name: outcreasegroup
parameters.label: New Group
parameters.description: Name of the group to place the generated creases into.

parameters.name: closeholes
parameters.label: Close Cracks
parameters.description: Choose how gaps are handled in the output geometry.

parameters.name: surroundpoly
parameters.label: Surrounding Faces
parameters.description: Choose how to handle the polygons on either side of a crack when pulling or stitching it closed.

parameters.name: bias
parameters.label: Bias
parameters.description: Determines which points are moved when pulling crack closed.

examples.label: Configure Depth and Override Crease Weight Attribute
examples.code:
n = op('subdivide1')
n.par.iterations = 4
n.par.overridecrease = False
n.par.surroundpoly = 'triangulate'
examples.description: Common settings from 4 real Derivative examples
